module.name: no-parent-internal-access
module.description: Disallow parent-relative access into protected internal directories such as src.

scenarios.name: should allow parent imports into non-protected directories
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../shared/foo' with default options
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import declaration

scenarios.name: should allow parent imports when the protected directory name is nested later in the path
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../shared/src/foo' with default options
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import declaration

scenarios.name: should allow protected-directory paths when the configured directory list does not include that name
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../src/foo' with protectedDirectories set to ['internal']
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import declaration

scenarios.name: should ignore protected-directory names that appear only after the first traversed segment
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../shared/src/foo' with protectedDirectories set to ['src']
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import declaration
scenarios.then: the path is matched only against the first non-parent segment and no src-specific violation is reported

scenarios.name: should report an import declaration that reaches into src through a parent path
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../src/foo' with default options
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import declaration

scenarios.name: should report a dynamic import that reaches into src through a parent path
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import expression

scenarios.name: should report a require call that reaches into a configured protected directory
scenarios.given: const foo = require('../internal/foo') with protectedDirectories set to ['internal']
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the require call

scenarios.name: should report a direct re-export that reaches into a protected directory through a parent path
scenarios.given: export { foo } from '../app/foo' with protectedDirectories set to ['app']
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the export declaration

scenarios.name: should report an import-equals declaration that reaches into src through a parent path
scenarios.given: import foo = require('../src/foo') with default options
scenarios.when: the rule evaluates the import-equals declaration

scenarios.name: should trim configured protected directory names before matching
scenarios.given: import { foo } from '../src/foo' with protectedDirectories set to ['  src  ', 'src', '   ']
scenarios.when: the rule resolves options and evaluates the import declaration
scenarios.then: empty entries are ignored, duplicate entries collapse, and a protectedParentImport violation is reported
